elements.text: ■ The Servicer does not make copies of original paper Mortgage file documents for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file documents (excluding the paper original Mortgage file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: noted above, which must always be maintained and stored as paper originals) using
elements.label: text

elements.text: microfilm or microfiche for Mortgages with Note Dates after June 30, 2013.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may copy original paper Mortgage file documents using scanning systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: and maintain copies of such documents as Portable Document Format (PDF), Tagged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Image File (TIF) format, Joint Photographic Experts Group (JPEG) format or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic document formats commonly used in the regular course of business.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Electronic Mortgage file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of the Servicer’s Mortgage files contain (i) some Electronic Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents that were paper and (ii) some Electronic Mortgage file documents that were
elements.label: text

elements.text: originally created as Electronic documents, as permitted by Chapter 1401 or elsewhere in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer’s Purchase Documents, the Servicer may convert to another Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: document format if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that it is a business necessity to convert such Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file documents to a different Electronic document format (e.g., due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: technological innovation, changes to the Servicer’s Systems, merger of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a Servicer that has different Systems or a Transfer of Servicing to a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: with different Systems), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The view (visual computer screen image) of and the content (data contained in or
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with the Electronic documents) in the Electronic Mortgage file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not altered, garbled, lost or destroyed
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Additional document retention and Document Custodian requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements set forth above, Servicers must comply with the document
elements.label: text

elements.text: retention and Document Custodian requirements set forth in Sections 9206.4(b),
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage file control and identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the form in which Mortgage files and records are kept, the Servicer must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: control and identification features in place to permit the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ready identification of the Freddie Mac loan number assigned to each Mortgage serviced
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ready identification of which Mortgages are MERS-registered and of those that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: closed with MERS as original mortgagee of record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prompt retrieval and, if applicable, delivery to Freddie Mac of a file or individual
elements.label: text

elements.text: components of a file by Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prompt preparation and delivery to Freddie Mac of scheduled and unscheduled reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: that Freddie Mac may require by Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, for any reason, Freddie Mac changes a loan number, the Servicer must upon notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: promptly make the necessary changes to the applicable Mortgage file and records to reflect
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new Freddie Mac loan number and instruct its Document Custodian to take similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: action.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain the Mortgage file while Freddie Mac retains an interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Mortgage and for at least seven years from the date Freddie Mac’s interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage was paid in full, the Mortgage file must contain a copy of the canceled Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage was repurchased by the Servicer to effectuate a Transfer of Ownership that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not allowed by or does not meet Freddie Mac’s requirements, the Mortgage file must contain
elements.label: text

elements.text: a copy of the executed Transfer of Ownership or assumption/release of liability instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3302.2: Security and contingency measures (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Damage or loss of Mortgage files
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of Mortgage files; security of Mortgage file information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Damage or loss of Mortgage files
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must bear the entire cost of promptly restoring Mortgage files and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents and records damaged or lost for any reason.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Transfer of Mortgage files; security of Mortgage file information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may at any time require the Servicer to deliver the following documents to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian approved by Freddie Mac or a transferee designated by Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any original Note, Security Instrument, assignment and modifying instrument still in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any Mortgage file, document within a Mortgage file or other related documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: records in the Servicer’s or its Document Custodian’s custody, whether maintained as
elements.label: text

elements.text: originals or as copies in accordance with Section 3302.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may, without Freddie Mac’s prior approval, entrust custody of all or part of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file to the Document Custodian holding Notes and assignments under Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2202.1(b). When requested, the Servicer must be able to identify to Freddie Mac those file
elements.label: text

elements.text: items held by the Document Custodian and document to Freddie Mac the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian’s acknowledgment that such file items:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Are Freddie Mac’s property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Will be maintained by the Document Custodian according to standards at least equal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: those set in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Will be maintained in such a way as to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Ensure the security and confidentiality of the information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Protect against anticipated threats or hazards to the security or integrity of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Protect against unauthorized access to or use of such information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Will be surrendered to Freddie Mac at any time Freddie Mac may request them
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to indemnify Freddie Mac and hold Freddie Mac harmless for any loss,
elements.label: text

elements.text: damage or expense (including court costs and reasonable attorney fees) that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may incur as a result of the Document Custodian’s holding all or part of the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain a copy (in a form allowable under Section 3302.1(b)) of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: original document that has been entrusted to the Document Custodian for safekeeping.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If all or part of the Mortgage file is held by the Servicer’s Document Custodian, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: agrees to recover from the Document Custodian (at the Servicer’s expense) and provide to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (at the place and within the time frame specified by Freddie Mac) any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian-held original document requested by Freddie Mac for the postfunding
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control detailed in Chapter 3301 or in conjunction with a Freddie Mac desktop or on￾site review of the Servicer’s Servicing operations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3401: Freddie Mac’s Postfunding Quality
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Control Review
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3401.1: Postfunding quality control processes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit enhancer postfunding review
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: As used in this Section 3401.1(a), the following terms have the meanings ascribed to them
elements.label: text

elements.text: below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Component Based
elements.label: text

elements.text: Review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s postfunding quality control review of certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: components of the Mortgage file. A Component Based Review does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not constitute a Full-File Review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correction Action taken by the Seller/Servicer, typically through delivery of
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation or information to Freddie Mac, that demonstrates that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the identified Significant Defect:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Did not, in fact, exist at the time of Mortgage purchase, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has been corrected in the time frame and manner specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents such that the Defect is no longer considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac to be a Significant Defect
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of Corrections include, but are not limited to, De Minimis
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections and the acquisition of required insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counterparty Status Freddie Mac’s assessment of a Seller/Servicer’s financial capacity
elements.label: text

elements.text: that could determine which Remedy Freddie Mac will offer to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer for a Mortgage with a Significant Defect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: D
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defect A loan-level deficiency that breaches a term contained in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents in effect at the time of Mortgage purchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: De Minimis
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correction
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minor amount not to exceed $500 (or such higher amount as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac may agree) that, when remitted,
elements.label: text

elements.text: refunded or otherwise provided, corrects or otherwise resolves an
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified Significant Defect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A De Minimis Correction cannot be made:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In connection with any Mortgage where the correction would
elements.label: text

elements.text: result in a specific required minimum Borrower contribution not
elements.label: text

elements.text: being met, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To correct a violation of the requirements set forth in the Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Charter Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: P
elements.label: text

elements.text: Price-Adjusted Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (PAL)
elements.label: text

elements.text: One or more Defects that, when considered with other Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: features and based on the facts of the Mortgage as delivered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, result in a Mortgage that was otherwise eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery to Freddie Mac had the correct data been delivered and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fees been paid to Freddie Mac by the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedy An action to resolve a Significant Defect elected by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the Purchase Documents in effect at the time of Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedies other than repurchase of the identified Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternatives could include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Performing loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Recourse/repurchase agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Indemnification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgage insurance stand-in agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Collateralized indemnification agreements (collateral in lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of repurchase or collateralized recourse)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Fee-only agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-performing loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Make-whole payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Split loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Loss reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: S
elements.label: text

elements.text: Significant Defect One or more Defects that either necessitate a change to the price for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Mortgage was purchased or result in the Mortgage being
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable for purchase had the true and accurate information
elements.label: text

elements.text: about the Mortgage been known at time of purchase. Mortgages with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Findings and PALs are not Mortgages with Significant Defects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In determining whether there is a Significant Defect, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: must give due consideration to the severity of the Defect. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in order to be a Significant Defect, the Defect must relate to one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The underwriting of the Borrower’s creditworthiness and capacity
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., Borrower’s income, Borrower credit/liabilities and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower assets) or Borrower eligibility and qualification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., Area Median Income, First-Time Homebuyer, lawful
elements.label: text

elements.text: presence in the United States)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The underwriting criteria related to property or project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., residential use and condominium eligibility), the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal or the physical condition of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage or product terms and criteria (e.g., products that may
elements.label: text

elements.text: require special Seller/Servicer approval as a prerequisite for
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery and the criteria described in the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents such as loan-to-value (LTV) ratio, occupancy, Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score, Mortgage purpose and Mortgage Product, and terms such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as ineligible transaction types and limitations on cash out to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that determines the type of refinance) or any terms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: criteria set forth in any negotiated provision)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A life-of-loan representation and warranty (as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1301.6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements applicable at time of Mortgage purchase (e.g., no
elements.label: text

elements.text: defaults, all taxes and insurances have been paid or escrows
elements.label: text

elements.text: established and no modification, encumbrance, subordination or
elements.label: text

elements.text: release of Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The warranties and obligations of Seller/Servicer set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 3101.1 regarding the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: available via Freddie Mac Gateway, or in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3101.2 regarding the FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The existence, sufficiency or enforceability of any required
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance or guaranty, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The form and/or execution of Freddie Mac-required Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents that without which make the Mortgage ineligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale or limit the enforceability of the required Mortgage terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., Uniform Residential Loan Application, power of attorney,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Texas 50(a)(6) Mortgage documents or nonstandard and special
elements.label: text

elements.text: purpose documents such as Living Trusts)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) General quality control policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to conduct a postfunding quality control review of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac. This review may be a Component Based Review or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Full-File Review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will underwrite each Mortgage selected for postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: review to verify that the Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is of investment quality (as defined in Section 4201.1),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is based on a determination that the Borrower has acceptable credit reputation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is secured by collateral that is adequate for the transaction, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Otherwise complies with Freddie Mac’s underwriting guidelines and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in the Purchase Documents, including compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable anti-predatory lending laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s determination of whether a Mortgage is of investment quality and
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise complies with all requirements of the Purchase Documents is conclusive,
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless the IDR Process described in Section 3602.4(b) is elected, in which case the
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision of the Neutral (as such term is defined in the IDR Document) shall be binding.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) FICO® scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac obtains FICO scores for Mortgages purchased and uses such scores and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: accompanying reason codes as one of the sampling criteria for postfunding quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: control reviews.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller delivers and documents an Indicator Score in accordance with the provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 5202.1, Freddie Mac will use the Seller-delivered score.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Reviewing Accept Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Accept Mortgages, the Borrower will be considered creditworthy and no further
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of Borrower creditworthiness will be made in the postfunding review if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has submitted to Loan Product Advisor® all required information and has
elements.label: text

elements.text: complied with the terms applicable to Accept Mortgages in the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The information submitted is true, complete, accurate and documented in the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: file
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Mortgage file submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify the Seller/Servicer via e-mail that certain Mortgages have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected for postfunding quality control review and will provide a checklist of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: that must be submitted to Freddie Mac for each selected Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise instructed by Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer must submit:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All credit, income, employment, collateral and asset documentation used by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: to make the Mortgage underwriting decision,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The documents listed in Sections 3401.2(a) through 3401.5(c), as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The source documentation for any Indicator Score,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other documents listed on the checklist, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If requested by Freddie Mac, any other Mortgage-related documents that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer is required to maintain in the Mortgage file, including any documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under the Guide or other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, for nonperforming Mortgages requested for postfunding quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: review, the checklist includes records of all collection efforts. This includes Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: contacts, property inspections, attempts to develop a workout plan and bankruptcy and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure tracking logs and notes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must provide all documents indicated on the applicable postfunding
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control checklist in an electronic media format and file size as specified on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Quality Control webpage located at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/general/specifications-for-electronic-file-delivery-for-qc￾loan-file-reviews.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right, at its sole discretion, to take appropriate action if a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer fails to deliver the required documents in the specified media format and
elements.label: text

elements.text: file size. Such action may include, but is not limited to, assessing a noncompliance fee to
elements.label: text

elements.text: cover costs incurred by manually processing file deliveries.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although all documentation sent to Freddie Mac may be considered in the postfunding
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control review, trended credit data (as described in Section 5202.1(c)) will not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered. The Seller/Servicer is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and authenticity
elements.label: text

elements.text: of all documentation in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac must receive requested Mortgage files, containing all specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation, within 30 days after the request for postfunding quality control review. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requested Mortgage file is not received within this time frame, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: issue a repurchase request. Failing to submit the Mortgage file as requested may result in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Seller’s or Servicer’s suspension or disqualification. However, Freddie Mac may, at its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion, specify a shorter or longer period for the receipt of the requested Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: files.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Finally, an offer by the Seller/Servicer to repurchase the Mortgage does not exempt them
elements.label: text

elements.text: from submitting the required documents for postfunding quality control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Process for remedying selling and origination defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The steps below describe the process that Freddie Mac will follow to categorize Defects,
elements.label: text

elements.text: allow Corrections to Defects and remedy Defects through either a repurchase request or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Identification of Defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: After a postfunding quality control review is completed, Freddie Mac will designate
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Defect(s) as resulting in one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Finding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A PAL
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Significant Defect
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Remedy letters will detail the specific Significant Defects found
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the quality control review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac designates the Defect as resulting in a Finding, Freddie Mac will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: require a Correction or a Remedy from the Seller/Servicer. However, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may request a data update from the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac designates the Defect as resulting in a PAL, the Seller/Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay to Freddie Mac the applicable Credit Fees that should have been paid when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage was purchased by Freddie Mac had the true and accurate facts about the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage been known at the time of purchase, which will be paid in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6303.2(a). Freddie Mac may not demand repurchase of a PAL.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Seller/Servicer may not voluntarily repurchase a PAL.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac identifies a Significant Defect, Freddie Mac will require repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage or may offer the Seller a Repurchase Alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac also checks for data discrepancies (which could be independent of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Full-File Review) that may result in the assessment of or an adjustment to a Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Correcting certain significant Defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the Appeal Process described in Section 3602.4(b), a Seller/Servicer has the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right to provide a Correction of any Significant Defects. Any additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation or information that the Seller/Servicer provides is subject to the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: standard of quality control review as the initial Mortgage file documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documentation submitted to correct a Significant Defect must be based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: information or data that either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Was available at the time of underwriting (and no later than the Note Date), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Covers the time of underwriting so long as such evidence meets the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation requirements set forth in the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the appeals process, Seller/Servicers have the ability to correct Defects related
elements.label: text

elements.text: to property, flood or mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer does not repurchase the Mortgage or perform any Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative in accordance with the Purchase Documents, such Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: continues to have the obligation to correct any Significant Defect in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Freddie Mac review of Seller response and Mortgage reassessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the Appeal Process described in Section 3602.4(b), Freddie Mac reassesses a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage by reviewing any Corrections submitted by the Seller/Servicer according to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable Purchase Documents to determine if a Significant Defect remains.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer provides additional documentation or information or takes
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary steps that correct all Significant Defects per the Purchase Documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will rescind the Remedy request. However, if after reassessment any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Significant Defects have not been corrected according to the Purchase Documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer must promptly comply with the Remedy request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the reassessment leads Freddie Mac to classify the Mortgage as a PAL, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must remit the applicable Credit Fees to Freddie Mac per Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6303.2. These fees reflect what should have been paid at the time of Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase had the true facts been known.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Throughout the appeals process, the Seller/Servicer can propose a Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative upon identification of a Significant Defect. Freddie Mac will consider this
elements.label: text

elements.text: proposal in good faith and respond accordingly. Nonetheless, Freddie Mac can offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: or decline Repurchase Alternatives depending on the Seller/Servicer’s Counterparty
elements.label: text

elements.text: Status, especially where future obligations are involved. Other factors for
elements.label: text

elements.text: consideration may include the Seller/Servicer’s quality of loan origination and their
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity and willingness to honor other Purchase Document provisions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage has multiple Significant Defects and the Seller/Servicer manages to
elements.label: text

elements.text: correct only some, Freddie Mac may pursue repurchase or other alternatives as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedy if Significant Defects persist after reassessment. Conversely, if all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Significant Defects are rectified, yet a defect still classifies the Mortgage as a PAL
elements.label: text

elements.text: post-reassessment, Freddie Mac will notify the Seller/Servicer of the due Credit Fee,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must be paid in alignment with Section 6303.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Range of possible outcomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Finding
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage has had a Full-File Review completed with only Findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: discovered, the Seller/Servicer will be relieved from Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcement of certain selling representations and warranties related to such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage (as described in Section 1301.6(a)), provided all other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1301.6 are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage has not had a Full-File Review, the Seller/Servicer may still be
elements.label: text

elements.text: relieved from Freddie Mac’s enforcement of certain selling representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties through other provisions under the selling representation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranty framework described in Section 1301.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) PAL
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appeals process for a PAL will result in one of the following outcomes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rescission of the request for the payment of the Credit Fee, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payment of the Credit Fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a PAL has undergone a Full-File Review and no Significant Defects have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been identified, the Seller/Servicer will be relieved from Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcement of certain selling representations and warranties related to that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage (as outlined in Section 1301.6(a)) once the Seller/Servicer pays the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fee and meets all other requirements specified in Section 1301.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conversely, for a PAL that has not had a Full-File Review, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: will not be relieved from Freddie Mac’s enforcement of the aforementioned
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling representations and warranties upon payment of the Credit Fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Seller/Servicer may still achieve relief from enforcement of
elements.label: text

elements.text: certain selling representations and warranties through other provisions within
elements.label: text

elements.text: the selling representation and warranty framework described in Section 1301.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C) Significant Defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appeals process for Mortgages with Significant Defects may result in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: range of outcomes, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rescission of the Remedy request,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Agreement on a Repurchase Alternative, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fulfillment of the Remedy request
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac offers a Repurchase Alternative after a repurchase demand has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been issued, the Seller/Servicer has the option to repurchase the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: instead of accepting the Repurchase Alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage with a Significant Defect has had a Full-File Review, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer will be relieved from Freddie Mac’s enforcement of certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling representations and warranties related to such Mortgage (as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1301.6(a)) if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All Significant Defects have been corrected, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Repurchase Alternative has been offered, accepted and fully executed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed by the Seller/Servicer in compliance with any related terms,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the expiration of all applicable time frames, provided all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in Section 1301.6 are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: At its sole discretion, Freddie Mac, in addition to its other contractual and legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: remedies, will require repurchase of each Mortgage that does not comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirement(s) set forth in the Purchase Documents to submit requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage documentation for postfunding quality control review within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: allotted time, unless the IDR Process is elected, in which case the decision of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Neutral (as such term is defined in the IDR Document) shall be binding.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Credit enhancer postfunding review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may choose to obtain a credit enhancement on some or all of the Mortgages it
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchases, either concurrently with or after the Mortgage purchase. In such cases, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller, as the Servicer, or any transferee Servicer may receive an electronic request from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to supply the credit enhancer with Mortgage files or other Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: information or documentation (“Mortgage information”) for the credit-enhanced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s request will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Identify the credit enhancer requesting the Mortgage information
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Authorize the release of specific Mortgage information
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Provide the name and contact details for the individual at the credit enhancer to whom
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage information should be directed
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller or transferee Servicer must provide the Mortgage information by either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 30 days after receiving the request, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The written deadline agreed upon between the credit enhancer and the Seller or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferee Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each request for Mortgage information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller or transferee Servicer will not receive notice regarding any action taken by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the credit enhancer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller or transferee Servicer fails to cooperate with the credit enhancer and this
elements.label: text

elements.text: jeopardizes Freddie Mac’s ability to maintain the credit enhancement, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may exercise any rights and remedies under the relevant Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requests for Mortgage information by a credit enhancer do not alter Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Purchase Documents, including the right to conduct a future quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: review of the Mortgages and to issue remedies for Mortgages Freddie Mac deems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ineligible. As outlined in Section 1301.5(d), the Seller’s representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: remain unaffected by investigations carried out by the credit enhancer or subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigations by, or on behalf of, Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3401.2: Documentation and instruments (02/04/26)

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modifying instrument or assumption of indebtedness agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Residential loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting explanation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property valuation documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal form rental information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance certificate
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: A legible copy of the Note, with all required accompanying documents (see the definition of
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Note” in the Glossary) and showing all endorsements, must be maintained in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file. The endorsement from the Seller, as required in Section 6301.2(a), does not need to
elements.label: text

elements.text: appear on the copy of the Note submitted for postfunding quality control review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by property in New York State that are documented using a New
elements.label: text

elements.text: York Consolidation, Extension and Modification Agreement (NY CEMA), the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: files must contain legible copies of the following documents:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consolidated Note showing all endorsements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Original Old Money Note and, if applicable, Original New Money (Gap) Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Current Form 3172 as specified in Section 4101.11(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Modifying instrument or assumption of indebtedness agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following documents must be included in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The modification or conversion agreement, or the ownership transfer instrument and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption of indebtedness agreement, if the Mortgage has been modified or converted
elements.label: text

elements.text: from an ARM to a fixed-rate Mortgage or if the ownership of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been transferred in any way.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Seller/Servicer does not need to submit for postfunding quality control review
elements.label: text

elements.text: a copy of any modifying instrument that by its terms ceases to be effective upon purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Construction to Permanent Modification Agreement for Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage application, credit report, employment and income verifications for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current Borrowers if the Mortgaged Premises is currently owned by anyone other than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original Borrowers. A certification of the terms of the sale of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., a copy of the purchase agreement signed by the seller and purchaser) must also be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: A completed Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary, or equivalent
elements.label: text

elements.text: form (e.g., a Feedback Certificate), must be included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as stated below, the Mortgage file must contain a copy of a title commitment for title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance on an American Land Title Association form (or its equivalent if a certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title or attorney opinion of title is being delivered), including all schedules and proposed
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements. The Mortgage file must also contain a copy of the final title insurance policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless it has not been received prior to the delivery of the loan to Freddie Mac. The final
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy, once issued, must be maintained in the Mortgage file and must include all required
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Cooperative Share Loans recognized as personal property and HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are leasehold Mortgages, the Mortgage file is not required to contain a copy of the title
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitment or final title insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 4702.6 and 4702.7 for title insurance requirements for Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans and HeritageOne Mortgages, respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Residential loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Record (as that term is defined in Section 1401.1(b)) of the initial Form 65, Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The final signed application, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Record of Form 1103, Supplemental Consumer Information Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: The final signed application must match the file documentation. If there are any
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancies between the information on the application and the information in the credit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: income and assets verifications, a written explanation of such discrepancies must also be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Underwriting explanation
elements.label: text

elements.text: When either the total monthly housing expense-to-income ratio guideline of 28% or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly debt payment-to-income ratio guideline of 36% is exceeded on Manually
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwritten Mortgages, a written explanation fully supporting the Seller’s underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Property valuation documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages for which the Seller is offered and accepts
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated collateral evaluation (ACE), or ACE+ PDR, in accordance with Section 5602.3 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5602.4, the Mortgage file must include all documentation used to determine that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is acceptable, including, but not limited to, an appraisal report and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion report for appraisals subject to conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Appraisal form rental information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental information on completed appraisal forms (i.e., Form 1000, Single Family
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable Rent Schedule, or Form 72, Small Residential Income Property Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report, as applicable), in accordance with the requirements of Chapter 5306, must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Leasehold Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgaged Premises is on a leasehold estate, a copy of the lease with recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: information must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with a temporary subsidy buydown plan, the Mortgage file must contain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the executed buydown agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Mortgage insurance certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: If mortgage insurance is required, a copy of the mortgage insurance certificate or electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence of mortgage insurance coverage, as well as all documents and information relating
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any change in mortgage insurance from that provided at delivery—including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, notices of cancelation, rescission or termination of mortgage insurance and denials
elements.label: text

elements.text: of claims—must be included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Documentation of flood zone determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: The flood zone determination must be documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m)Evidence of flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If flood insurance is required, the Mortgage file must include acceptable final evidence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: flood insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If final evidence of flood insurance is not available at the time of the quality control review,
elements.label: text

elements.text: one of the following documents is acceptable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and executed National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) Flood Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Application plus a copy of the Borrower’s premium check or agent’s paid receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and executed NFIP Flood Insurance Application plus the final
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement reflecting the flood insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and executed NFIP General Change Endorsement Form showing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment of the current flood insurance policy by the property seller to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the flood insurer is not the NFIP, the insurer’s equivalent of the applicable NFIP form is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the flood zone determination documentation shows the insurable improvements are in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Special Flood Hazard Area but flood insurance was waived, the Mortgage file must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: the documentation which served as the basis for the waiver.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage that is concurrently originated with a subordinate lien (i.e., the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and subordinate lien are originated on the same day), the Seller must include in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file a copy of the following documentation for the subordinate lien:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note or other evidence of subordinate lien terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement or an alternative form required by law that
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidences the fees and costs paid by the Borrower at closing in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs), the HELOC agreement indicating all fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: and costs paid by the Borrower at closing and the maximum permitted credit advance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For refinance Mortgages, the Mortgage file must include evidence of subordination of
elements.label: text

elements.text: outstanding secondary financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (o) Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage is originated using any Electronic Records (as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: expressly permitted by Freddie Mac in the Guide or in a Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, the Seller/Servicer’s Mortgage file may contain a combination of paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents and Electronic Records and must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The clear and conspicuous statement of certain information required by E-Sign (see Title
elements.label: text

elements.text: I, Section 101(c)(1)(B)(i)-(iv) and (C)(i) of E-Sign) and satisfactory evidence that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower received the information before consenting to Electronic Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The “Consent to Electronic Records” required by E-Sign (see Title I, Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 101(c)(1)(A) and (C)(i)-(ii) of E-Sign) and satisfactory evidence that “reasonably
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrates” that the Borrower was able to access the information required by E-Sign
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see preceding bullet) before electronically consenting or confirming consent to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Federal or State disclosures that were electronically presented to and accessed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower electronically, with satisfactory evidence of when and how the electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosures were received and, if required, signed by the Borrower using an Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature (as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other paper documents and/or Electronic Records required by the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, with satisfactory evidence of when and how the paper documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or Electronic Records were received and, if required, signed by the Borrower either
elements.label: text

elements.text: in writing or using an Electronic Signature, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: In all cases, the paper documents and/or Electronic Records (including Electronic Signatures)
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file must be sufficient to permit Freddie Mac to perform a postfunding
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control review and, among other things, obtain a reverification of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment and income from employers and/or other third parties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer’s Mortgage files contain a combination of paper documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records, the Seller/Servicer must maintain a computerized tracking system that
elements.label: text

elements.text: cross-references the locations of both paper documents and Electronic Records for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. Such computerized information must be made available to Freddie Mac promptly
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (p) Other documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees to maintain in the Mortgage file and submit to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The most recent Feedback Certificate and responses, including Risk Class results and
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All documents necessary to evidence compliance with all applicable anti-predatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: lending laws and regulations, including, for example, any required reasonable tangible
elements.label: text

elements.text: net benefit analysis, Borrower disclosures or disclosures relating to affiliated business or
elements.label: text

elements.text: service providers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All other documents required under the Guide or other Purchase Documents, requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac or otherwise commonly maintained in files of private institutional
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage investors or servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may require that the requested documents be delivered to Freddie Mac, an agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac, or to a Document Custodian.
elements.label: text
