elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage application, credit report, employment and income verifications for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current Borrowers if the Mortgaged Premises is currently owned by anyone other than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original Borrowers. A certification of the terms of the sale of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., a copy of the purchase agreement signed by the seller and purchaser) must also be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: A completed Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary, or equivalent
elements.label: text

elements.text: form (e.g., a Feedback Certificate), must be included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as stated below, the Mortgage file must contain a copy of a title commitment for title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance on an American Land Title Association form (or its equivalent if a certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title or attorney opinion of title is being delivered), including all schedules and proposed
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements. The Mortgage file must also contain a copy of the final title insurance policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless it has not been received prior to the delivery of the loan to Freddie Mac. The final
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy, once issued, must be maintained in the Mortgage file and must include all required
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Cooperative Share Loans recognized as personal property and HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are leasehold Mortgages, the Mortgage file is not required to contain a copy of the title
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitment or final title insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 4702.6 and 4702.7 for title insurance requirements for Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans and HeritageOne Mortgages, respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Residential loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Record (as that term is defined in Section 1401.1(b)) of the initial Form 65, Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The final signed application, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Record of Form 1103, Supplemental Consumer Information Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: The final signed application must match the file documentation. If there are any
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancies between the information on the application and the information in the credit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: income and assets verifications, a written explanation of such discrepancies must also be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Underwriting explanation
elements.label: text

elements.text: When either the total monthly housing expense-to-income ratio guideline of 28% or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly debt payment-to-income ratio guideline of 36% is exceeded on Manually
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwritten Mortgages, a written explanation fully supporting the Seller’s underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Property valuation documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages for which the Seller is offered and accepts
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated collateral evaluation (ACE), or ACE+ PDR, in accordance with Section 5602.3 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5602.4, the Mortgage file must include all documentation used to determine that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is acceptable, including, but not limited to, an appraisal report and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion report for appraisals subject to conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Appraisal form rental information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental information must be completed on the appraisal report in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Chapter 5306, as applicable, and the appraisal report must be included in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Leasehold Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgaged Premises is on a leasehold estate, a copy of the lease with recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: information must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with a temporary subsidy buydown plan, the Mortgage file must contain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the executed buydown agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Mortgage insurance certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: If mortgage insurance is required, a copy of the mortgage insurance certificate or electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence of mortgage insurance coverage, as well as all documents and information relating
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any change in mortgage insurance from that provided at delivery—including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, notices of cancelation, rescission or termination of mortgage insurance and denials
elements.label: text

elements.text: of claims—must be included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Documentation of flood zone determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: The flood zone determination must be documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m)Evidence of flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If flood insurance is required, the Mortgage file must include acceptable final evidence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: flood insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If final evidence of flood insurance is not available at the time of the quality control review,
elements.label: text

elements.text: one of the following documents is acceptable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and executed National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) Flood Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Application plus a copy of the Borrower’s premium check or agent’s paid receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and executed NFIP Flood Insurance Application plus the final
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement reflecting the flood insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and executed NFIP General Change Endorsement Form showing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment of the current flood insurance policy by the property seller to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the flood insurer is not the NFIP, the insurer’s equivalent of the applicable NFIP form is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the flood zone determination documentation shows the insurable improvements are in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Special Flood Hazard Area but flood insurance was waived, the Mortgage file must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: the documentation which served as the basis for the waiver.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage that is concurrently originated with a subordinate lien (i.e., the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and subordinate lien are originated on the same day), the Seller must include in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file a copy of the following documentation for the subordinate lien:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note or other evidence of subordinate lien terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement or an alternative form required by law that
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidences the fees and costs paid by the Borrower at closing in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs), the HELOC agreement indicating all fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: and costs paid by the Borrower at closing, and the maximum permitted credit advance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For refinance Mortgages, the Mortgage file must include evidence of subordination of
elements.label: text

elements.text: outstanding secondary financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (o) Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage is originated using any Electronic Records (as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: expressly permitted by Freddie Mac in the Guide or in a Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, the Seller/Servicer’s Mortgage file may contain a combination of paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents and Electronic Records and must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The clear and conspicuous statement of certain information required by E-Sign (see Title
elements.label: text

elements.text: I, Section 101(c)(1)(B)(i)-(iv) and (C)(i) of E-Sign) and satisfactory evidence that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower received the information before consenting to Electronic Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The “Consent to Electronic Records” required by E-Sign (see Title I, Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 101(c)(1)(A) and (C)(i)-(ii) of E-Sign) and satisfactory evidence that “reasonably
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrates” that the Borrower was able to access the information required by E-Sign
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see preceding bullet) before electronically consenting or confirming consent to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Federal or State disclosures that were electronically presented to and accessed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower electronically, with satisfactory evidence of when and how the electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosures were received and, if required, signed by the Borrower using an Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature (as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other paper documents and/or Electronic Records required by the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, with satisfactory evidence of when and how the paper documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or Electronic Records were received and, if required, signed by the Borrower either
elements.label: text

elements.text: in writing or using an Electronic Signature, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: In all cases, the paper documents and/or Electronic Records (including Electronic Signatures)
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file must be sufficient to permit Freddie Mac to perform a postfunding
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control review and, among other things, obtain a reverification of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment and income from employers and/or other third parties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer’s Mortgage files contain a combination of paper documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records, the Seller/Servicer must maintain a computerized tracking system that
elements.label: text

elements.text: cross-references the locations of both paper documents and Electronic Records for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. Such computerized information must be made available to Freddie Mac promptly
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (p) Other documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees to maintain in the Mortgage file and submit to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The most recent Feedback Certificate and responses, including Risk Class results and
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All documents necessary to evidence compliance with all applicable anti-predatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: lending laws and regulations, including, for example, any required reasonable tangible
elements.label: text

elements.text: net benefit analysis, Borrower disclosures or disclosures relating to affiliated business or
elements.label: text

elements.text: service providers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All other documents required under the Guide or other Purchase Documents, requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac or otherwise commonly maintained in files of private institutional
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage investors or servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may require that the requested documents be delivered to Freddie Mac, an agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac, or to a Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3401.3: Credit documentation (03/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verification of employment and income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verification of source of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verification of previous Mortgage/rental payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Credit documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A credit report must be included in the Mortgage file. If Credit Scores were obtained for
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting, they must be included in the Mortgage file. For any Mortgage underwritten
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Credit Scores, the Credit Score selected for each Borrower and the process used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: select the Credit Score must be noted on Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Summary, or another similar document in the Mortgage file. Source documentation also must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Verification of employment and income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation of employment and income verification and a written analysis, when
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, of the income used to qualify the Borrower must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must include a payment history from the first payment Due Date to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Date. This Mortgage payment history must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Demonstrate no Delinquencies of 30 days or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be understandable without code translation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Verification of source of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must include evidence of verification of all eligible sources of funds used
elements.label: text

elements.text: to qualify the Borrower for the Mortgage transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Verification of previous Mortgage/rental payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evidence of verification of Mortgage payment history and/or rental payment history for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12-month period prior to the date of the Mortgage application must be included in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file for Manually Underwritten Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3401.4: Sales contract (11/19/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase Mortgages, a copy of the fully executed sales contract and all addenda must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must include the final Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: related documentation evidencing all costs to the homebuyer and property seller, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3401.5: Specialty Mortgages and other considerations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ GreenCHOICE Mortgages®
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home, the Mortgage file must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation evidencing that the Manufactured Home is legally classified as real property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home is properly titled and the lien on the Manufactured Home is properly
elements.label: text

elements.text: created, evidenced and perfected.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage on a new Manufactured Home, the Mortgage file must contain a copy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: manufacturer’s invoice and Manufactured Home Purchase Agreement in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Manufactured Home is a new installation, the Mortgage file must contain evidence that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home was installed in compliance with applicable HUD Codes for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes (HUD Codes).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Community Land Trust Mortgages, the Mortgage file must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If applicable, written approval signed by the Community Land Trust or its authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: representative permitting the Borrower to enter into a refinance transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An original executed or certified copy of the Community Land Trust ground lease and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 490, Community Land Trust Ground Lease Rider, containing the recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) GreenCHOICE Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For GreenCHOICE Mortgages, the Mortgage file must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of the eligible improvements, as described in Section 4606.1(b), and their
elements.label: text

elements.text: costs, such as receipts and/or invoices, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of the costs used to calculate the total acquisition cost for purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction Mortgages as referenced in Section 4606.2(a),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Manually Underwritten Mortgages that used one or both of the higher qualifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratios, as referenced in Section 5401.1(e)(ii)(B), documentation that the property has a
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of energy efficiency greater than that of a “standard” (i.e., non-energy efficient)
elements.label: text

elements.text: property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Mortgages used to finance eligible improvements completed after the Note Date, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: written escrow agreement as referenced in Section 4606.3(a)(3),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If applicable, an energy report or alternative documentation in lieu of an energy report,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If applicable, a completion report provided by the appraiser, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If proceeds were used to pay an Existing Debt (as defined in Section 4606.1(c)),
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation meeting the requirements in Section 4606.3(b), as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3402: Seller’s In-House Quality Control
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3402.1: Quality control program structure (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller’s quality control program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of third-party quality control services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Seller’s quality control program
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must operate a quality control program for Home Mortgages that is acceptable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. Organizations with a commitment to quality control recognize that quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: begins before an application is taken and continues throughout the entire Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The purpose of a quality control program is to monitor and evaluate the integrity of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination process and to provide feedback to the organization about its originations. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: feedback is used to adjust and improve production processes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective quality control programs are designed to monitor and evaluate the specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics of the Seller’s organization and reflect:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The size and structure of the organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The experience and expertise of the staff
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The geographic areas of operation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The branch structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The volume and types of Mortgages originated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The origination sources used (e.g., from Mortgage Brokers or Correspondents or via the
elements.label: text

elements.text: internet)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any significant changes in the product lines, origination sources or production process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Controls in place to ensure that internal policies and procedures are followed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although no single specific quality control program can meet the needs of all Sellers, there
elements.label: text

elements.text: are certain characteristics found in all effective quality control programs. The Seller’s quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: control program must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be in writing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for standard operating procedures for all employees who will be involved with or
elements.label: text

elements.text: affected by the quality control process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be capable of evaluating and monitoring the overall quality of Mortgage production on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: regular and timely basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include both:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Preclosing quality control reviews, and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Post-closing quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include procedures to ensure timely:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Sample selection,
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Mortgage file reviews, and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Reporting of findings to Senior Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may use any combination of preclosing or post-closing quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on its specific operations and needs. The Seller’s post-closing in-house quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must operate independently of the Mortgage origination and underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: departments. However, for Sellers with annual production of less than 5,000 Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, the post-closing quality control function may be housed with the origination and
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting departments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must also comply with the requirements of Section 3201.1(c) relating to fraud
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevention and detection in its quality control program, as well as other applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Chapter 3201 relating to fraud prevention, detection and reporting.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A variety of reference guides, tools and technology resources designed to assist
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers with their in-house quality control program can be found on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: website at https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/selling-delivery/delivery-options￾pricing/quality-control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Use of third-party quality control services
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may use third-party quality control services for all or part of its quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: program. The Seller using third-party quality control services must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the services performed by third-party quality control services comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitor and evaluate the performance of third-party quality control services on a regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must review third-party quality control findings and take the same corrective
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions as it would with respect to quality control findings made by its own staff.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s post-closing quality control program must include policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: addressing Transfers of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Servicing is transferred, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retain copies of the file documents to complete the quality control reviews, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Make arrangements with the new Servicer to assist in the quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records of completed quality control reviews must be provided to the new Servicer upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3402.2: Quality control processes (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-closing sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reverifications made by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data integrity review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preclosing quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Post-closing sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s post-closing quality control sample must, at a minimum, consist of three sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: types:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Random
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Targeted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Discretionary
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Random sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Mortgages in the random sample are randomly selected from the population so that
elements.label: text

elements.text: every Mortgage has an equal chance of selection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages must make up a representative portion of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control sample.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as provided under the last paragraph of this section, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Select at least 10% of one of the following production populations for quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: review:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Total annual Home Mortgage production
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Total annual secondary market Home Mortgage production
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Total annual Freddie Mac Home Mortgage production
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, any Mortgages excluded from the Seller’s quality control sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, as set forth in the remaining provisions of this section, are not eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Schedule its sampling procedures so that every Home Mortgage within the selected
elements.label: text

elements.text: population has a chance of being selected for review within 90 days of the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assign to quality control personnel the authority to conduct additional reviews at their
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Warrant that over the course of each 12-month period, the selected samples are
elements.label: text

elements.text: representative of the full scope of the Seller’s product line and production process
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the selected population
elements.label: text

elements.text: The full scope of the Seller’s product line and origination process includes all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from all product lines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from all States of operation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from each branch office
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from each third party involved in the origination process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages with high-risk characteristics (e.g., high loan-to-value ratios,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs, 3- to 4-unit properties, Manufactured Homes, cash-out refinance Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Property Mortgages and Caution Mortgages)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller with a total annual production volume in excess of 5,000 Home Mortgages may
elements.label: text

elements.text: substitute a statistically based sampling methodology that is of sufficient size to ensure a
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidence level of 95% and a margin of error not to exceed 2% on an annual basis based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the defect rates for Mortgages recently reviewed by the Seller’s quality control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Targeted sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each month, the Seller must select an appropriate risk-based sample of Mortgages sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac that become 60 days or more past due in the first six months following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These Mortgages must be carefully evaluated to determine the presence of any fraud or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Discretionary sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in a discretionary sample are selected on a non-random basis from a specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: population.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When selecting Mortgages to be reviewed as part of the Seller’s discretionary post￾closing quality control sample, the Seller may choose to make targeted Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: selections that focus on a specific characteristic of the Mortgage, such as product,
elements.label: text

elements.text: business source or underwriting component (e.g., income and employment, assets, credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: or property).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When conducting these discretionary reviews, the Seller must consider the purpose of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: targeted selection when determining whether certain reverifications are necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the purpose of the targeted selection is to focus specifically on income calculations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then reverification of assets or a review of the appraisal is not within the scope of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: review and is not required to be completed; however, reverifications of income and
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment are required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the purpose of the targeted selection is to review Mortgages originated through a
elements.label: text

elements.text: new source of business, then all aspects of the Mortgage are within the scope of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: review, and all reverifications noted in Section 3402.2(b) must be performed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As required in Section 3201.1(c), discretionary samples must be selected to evaluate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: work of a particular employee or Mortgage transaction participant when there is a reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: to suspect fraud. Discretionary samples should also be selected as needed in order to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review the work of a new branch office, employee or third-party originator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Validate that a new product or offering is being originated in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with a request from Freddie Mac to review loans in a specific population
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reverifications made by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must make certain reverifications on Loan Product Advisor and Non-Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor Mortgages selected for post-closing quality control review. The purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the reverification process include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluating the validity, accuracy and quality of the information used in the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protecting the Seller against fraud and misrepresentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers should begin reverifying the information listed in this section as soon as possible after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sample selection to facilitate the Mortgage file review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reverifications may be in written or verbal form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All reverification documentation must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Preclosing quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s quality control program must include a process to perform quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: reviews on a sampling of its Mortgages prior to closing. The goal of an effective preclosing
elements.label: text

elements.text: review process is to monitor the Seller’s origination policies, ensure the accuracy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage data and prevent the closing of Mortgages with deficiencies such as fraud,
elements.label: text

elements.text: inaccurate data and insufficient documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s in-house preclosing quality control process should operate independently of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage origination and underwriting departments when operationally possible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s preclosing review process should include procedures for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completing sample selection and ensuring timing permits reviews to be completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting deficiencies and taking appropriate corrective measures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting the resolution of defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Canceling or postponing settlement when the preclosing review reveals deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: or when the review cannot be completed prior to the scheduled settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s sampling process should include Mortgages that are representative of the full
elements.label: text

elements.text: scope of the Seller’s product line and production process as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3402.2(a)(i). The Seller should regularly assess its sampling methodology to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: its preclosing quality control process is effective. Additionally, the Seller should target
elements.label: text

elements.text: samples, as needed, in order to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review the work originated by a new branch office, employee or third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: originator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Validate that a new product or offering is being originated in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate the work of a particular employee or Mortgage transaction participant when
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is a reason to suspect fraud as required in Section 3201.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Validation and reverification
elements.label: text

elements.text: An effective preclosing quality control review process should include validation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: reverification of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data entered into Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Social Security number provided by each Borrower, unless the Seller validated the
elements.label: text

elements.text: number during the loan origination process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income documentation and calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assets required to close or meet reserves requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property valuation documentation or property valuation data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance commitment reflecting adequate coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether additional credit was granted and considered in qualifying when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s credit report reveals inquiries within the previous 90-day period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 3402.2(b) and 3402.2(d) for reverification exceptions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3402.2: Quality control processes (Future effective
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Post-closing sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reverifications made by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data integrity review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preclosing quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Post-closing sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s post-closing quality control sample must, at a minimum, consist of three sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: types:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Random
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Targeted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Discretionary
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Random sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Mortgages in the random sample are randomly selected from the population so that
elements.label: text

elements.text: every Mortgage has an equal chance of selection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages must make up a representative portion of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control sample.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as provided under the last paragraph of this section, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Select at least 10% of one of the following production populations for quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: review:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Total annual Home Mortgage production
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Total annual secondary market Home Mortgage production
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Total annual Freddie Mac Home Mortgage production
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, any Mortgages excluded from the Seller’s quality control sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, as set forth in the remaining provisions of this section, are not eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Schedule its sampling procedures so that every Home Mortgage within the selected
elements.label: text

elements.text: population has a chance of being selected for review within 90 days of the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assign to quality control personnel the authority to conduct additional reviews at their
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Warrant that over the course of each 12-month period, the selected samples are
elements.label: text

elements.text: representative of the full scope of the Seller’s product line and production process
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the selected population
elements.label: text

elements.text: The full scope of the Seller’s product line and origination process includes all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from all product lines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from all States of operation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from each branch office
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages from each third party involved in the origination process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Mortgages with high-risk characteristics (e.g., high loan-to-value ratios,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs, 3- to 4-unit properties, Manufactured Homes, cash-out refinance Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Property Mortgages and Caution Mortgages)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller with a total annual production volume in excess of 5,000 Home Mortgages may
elements.label: text

elements.text: substitute a statistically based sampling methodology that is of sufficient size to ensure a
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidence level of 95% and a margin of error not to exceed 2% on an annual basis based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the defect rates for Mortgages recently reviewed by the Seller’s quality control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Targeted sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each month, the Seller must select an appropriate risk-based sample of Mortgages sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac that become 60 days or more past due in the first six months following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These Mortgages must be carefully evaluated to determine the presence of any fraud or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other deficiency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Discretionary sample
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in a discretionary sample are selected on a non-random basis from a specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: population.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When selecting Mortgages to be reviewed as part of the Seller’s discretionary post￾closing quality control sample, the Seller may choose to make targeted Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: selections that focus on a specific characteristic of the Mortgage, such as product,
elements.label: text

elements.text: business source or underwriting component (e.g., income and employment, assets, credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: or property).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When conducting these discretionary reviews, the Seller must consider the purpose of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: targeted selection when determining whether certain reverifications are necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the purpose of the targeted selection is to focus specifically on income calculations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then reverification of assets or a review of the appraisal is not within the scope of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: review and is not required to be completed; however, reverifications of income and
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment are required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the purpose of the targeted selection is to review Mortgages originated through a
elements.label: text

elements.text: new source of business, then all aspects of the Mortgage are within the scope of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: review, and all reverifications noted in Section 3402.2(b) must be performed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As required in Section 3201.1(c), discretionary samples must be selected to evaluate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: work of a particular employee or Mortgage transaction participant when there is a reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: to suspect fraud. Discretionary samples should also be selected as needed in order to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review the work of a new branch office, employee or third-party originator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Validate that a new product or offering is being originated in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with a request from Freddie Mac to review loans in a specific population
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reverifications made by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must make certain reverifications on Loan Product Advisor and Non-Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor Mortgages selected for post-closing quality control review. The purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the reverification process include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluating the validity, accuracy and quality of the information used in the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protecting the Seller against fraud and misrepresentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers should begin reverifying the information listed in this section as soon as possible after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sample selection to facilitate the Mortgage file review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reverifications may be in written or verbal form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All reverification documentation must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text
