elements.text: and W-2 form(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must include the following applicable closing documents in its post-closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality control review to ensure that the information is accurate, complete and consistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with other documents in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notes and riders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security Instruments and assignments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance certificate or policy or Mortgage guaranty certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modification or assumption agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title binder or final title insurance policy (both if available) or other evidence of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Plat or survey
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statements and related documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estate documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hazard insurance policy or certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Flood insurance policy or certificate or flood zone determination documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriter’s approval and any conditions of closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closing instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Preclosing quality control reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s quality control program must include a process to perform quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: reviews on a sampling of its Mortgages prior to closing. The goal of an effective preclosing
elements.label: text

elements.text: review process is to monitor the Seller’s origination policies, ensure the accuracy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage data and prevent the closing of Mortgages with deficiencies such as fraud,
elements.label: text

elements.text: inaccurate data and insufficient documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s in-house preclosing quality control process should operate independently of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage origination and underwriting departments when operationally possible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s preclosing review process should include procedures for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completing sample selection and ensuring timing permits reviews to be completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting deficiencies and taking appropriate corrective measures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting the resolution of defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Canceling or postponing settlement when the preclosing review reveals deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: or when the review cannot be completed prior to the scheduled settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Sample selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s sampling process should include Mortgages that are representative of the full
elements.label: text

elements.text: scope of the Seller’s product line and production process as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3402.2(a)(i). The Seller should regularly assess its sampling methodology to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: its preclosing quality control process is effective. Additionally, the Seller should target
elements.label: text

elements.text: samples, as needed, in order to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review the work originated by a new branch office, employee or third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: originator
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Validate that a new product or offering is being originated in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate the work of a particular employee or Mortgage transaction participant when
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is a reason to suspect fraud as required in Section 3201.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Validation and reverification
elements.label: text

elements.text: An effective preclosing quality control review process should include validation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: reverification of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data entered into Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Social Security number provided by each Borrower, unless the Seller validated the
elements.label: text

elements.text: number during the loan origination process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income documentation and calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assets required to close or meet reserves requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property valuation documentation or property valuation data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance commitment reflecting adequate coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether additional credit was granted and considered in qualifying when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s credit report reveals inquiries within the previous 90-day period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 3402.2(b) and 3402.2(d) for reverification exceptions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3402.3: Documenting and reporting (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retention of Seller’s quality control records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Documenting reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain complete records for each Mortgage file selected for a preclosing or a post￾closing quality control review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document and explain discrepancies or inconsistencies found in the Mortgage file that
elements.label: text

elements.text: affect the eligibility of the Mortgage based on the requirements of the Seller, the MI or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s quality control program must provide that all preclosing and post-closing quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: control activities be fully documented in writing and reviewed by management on a regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting results
elements.label: text

elements.text: Quality control review results must be reported in writing to the Seller’s Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management within 90 days of selection of the Mortgage files for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must thoroughly analyze findings affecting the acceptability or eligibility of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and initiate any necessary corrective actions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Notification to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must notify Freddie Mac within 30 days of the date the quality control results
elements.label: text

elements.text: are reported in writing to the Seller’s Senior Management when a finding affects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility of a Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac or if the finding is related to fraud or
elements.label: text

elements.text: possible fraud.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must notify Freddie Mac Quality Control via the Tip Referral Tool, accessible
elements.label: text

elements.text: via Freddie Mac Gateway®, of findings that relate to any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Misrepresentations, misstatements or omissions identified in Section 3201.2(a), to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: extent they are associated with the origination of a Mortgage, whether discovered
elements.label: text

elements.text: through a post-closing quality control review or by any other means. All other
elements.label: text

elements.text: findings related to fraud, suspected fraud or other Suspicious Activity must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported in accordance with Section 3201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The underwriting of the Borrower’s creditworthiness and capacity (e.g., Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, credit/liabilities, assets) or Borrower eligibility and qualification (e.g., Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: Median Income, First-Time Homebuyer, lawful presence in the United States)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The underwriting criteria related to property or project eligibility (e.g., residential use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium eligibility), the appraisal report or the physical condition of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage or program terms and criteria (e.g., program that may require special
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer approval as a prerequisite for delivery and the criteria described in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, such as loan-to-value ratio, occupancy, Credit Score, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: purpose and Mortgage Product, terms such as ineligible transaction types and
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitations on cash out to the Borrower that determines the type of refinance) or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms and criteria set forth in any negotiated provision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A life-of-loan representation and warranty (as described in Section 1301.6) regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Charter matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Misstatements, misrepresentations and omissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Data inaccuracies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Clear title/First Lien priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Compliance with laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Unacceptable Mortgage products
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements applicable at the time the Mortgage was sold to Freddie Mac (e.g., no
elements.label: text

elements.text: defaults, all taxes and insurances have been paid or escrows have been established, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification, encumbrance, subordination or release of the Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The existence, sufficiency or enforceability of any required insurance or guaranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The form and/or execution of Freddie Mac required Mortgage documents, without
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Mortgage would be ineligible for sale to Freddie Mac or the enforceability
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the required Mortgage terms would be limited (e.g., Form 65, power of attorney,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgage documents, nonstandard or special purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents such as Living Trusts)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac reserves the right to increase the sampling or to impose other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements on a case-by-case basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Retention of Seller’s quality control records
elements.label: text

elements.text: For at least three years from the date of the preclosing or post-closing quality control review,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must retain all records of its quality control findings, along with documentation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any corrective action taken.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These records must be made available to Freddie Mac and upon request to the new Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: if a Transfer of Servicing occurs. Upon request by Freddie Mac, the Seller must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: records that clearly indicate:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Freddie Mac loan number,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller’s final determination (from its quality control department) of whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is an Investment Quality Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other loan-level information that is requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3403: Servicer’s Quality Control
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3403.1: Servicer’s quality control program (02/11/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Servicer must implement a quality control program with respect to its Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages for Freddie Mac, and, in implementing this program, it may leverage its existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes. Freddie Mac may review and require changes to a Servicer’s quality control program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have written policies and procedures documenting its quality control program’s requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consistently monitor compliance with these policies and procedures as part of a prudent risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: management framework
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Regularly review and assess the adequacy of its internal controls, procedures and systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: used in connection with the Servicing of Freddie Mac Mortgages to ensure compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide and other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must take remedial steps, as appropriate, to address any deficiencies identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of whether such deficiencies are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Discovered by the Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identified and communicated by Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identified by a Borrower and confirmed by the Servicer, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identified by a third party (e.g., external auditor, law firm, regulatory agency) and confirmed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must formally document the results of its reviews and assessments and make the
elements.label: text

elements.text: results, including any remediation plan or completed remediation, available to Freddie Mac upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must provide evidence of its quality control program upon Freddie Mac’s request. At
elements.label: text

elements.text: a minimum, the program must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Comply with the fraud prevention and detection requirements of Section 3201.1(c), as well
elements.label: text

elements.text: as other applicable fraud prevention, detection and reporting requirements of Chapter 3201
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Evaluate whether the Servicer is maintaining accurate and complete records as required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3302 and the document retention and Document Custodian requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Establish control and identification features for all Mortgage files, in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3302.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Provide for periodic reviews of the Servicer’s cash accounting, investor reporting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: remitting, escrow management and notifications to Borrowers, portfolio reconciliation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodial accounts management processes and controls, using a Mortgage sample size that
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflects a meaningful representation of the UPB of the Mortgages the Servicer services for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Assess ARM adjustment accuracy and notifications to Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Periodically perform quality control reviews or audits on any authorized Outsourced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vendor’s use of any Servicing Tool to ensure compliance with the Guide and other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Evaluate whether its operations personnel and, if applicable, Outsourced Vendors are
elements.label: text

elements.text: complying with and accurately conducting the preforeclosure referral Mortgage file review
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Sections 9101.1(e), 9102.4(b) and 9301.2(a) and preforeclosure sale account
elements.label: text

elements.text: review requirements of Section 9301.7(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Periodically review notices of default, notices of acceleration, right to cure notices and,
elements.label: text

elements.text: where applicable, right to appeal a loan modification denial notice to ensure that they are
elements.label: text

elements.text: properly dated, timely sent and otherwise provide Borrowers with all rights required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law and assess whether all condition precedents to proceeding with foreclosure are
elements.label: text

elements.text: met
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Periodically review compliance with Freddie Mac’s loss mitigation requirements, which
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes compliance with Freddie Mac’s modification program requirements and short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Maintain policies and procedures that are reasonably designed to ensure that the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigates, responds to and makes corrections in response to complaints or errors asserted
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3501: Freddie Mac Servicer Success File
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Reviews and Performance Criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3501.1: Freddie Mac Servicer Success File Reviews
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Types of File Reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File Review requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review conclusions and appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: As part of the Servicing Success Program, as described at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/content/_assets/resources/pdf/step-by-step￾guides/serv_success_prog.pdf, Freddie Mac will be conducting reviews of delinquent Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: files from time to time as discussed below. These file reviews (“Servicer Success File Reviews”
elements.label: text

elements.text: or “File Reviews”) are in addition to any audit of Mortgage files specified in Section 8101.4(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Types of File Reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify a Servicer in writing that certain Mortgages have been selected for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Success File Review. Freddie Mac may conduct the following types of File
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reviews:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prudent Servicing Review: An assessment of the Servicer’s collection activities, loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation activities, timeline management and property preservation processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short Sale Compliance Review: An assessment of the Servicer’s compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide and other Purchase Documents, as applicable, regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Modification Compliance Review: An assessment of the Servicer’s compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements of the Guide and other Purchase Documents, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding completed modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may modify or expand the types of File Reviews from time to time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) File Review requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify a Servicer in writing that certain Mortgages have been selected for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: File Review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the instructions and any requirements set forth in a File Review
elements.label: text

elements.text: request from Freddie Mac, which will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include the Freddie Mac loan numbers associated with the Mortgage files being
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specify the documentation that must be included with each type of file (e.g., short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Chapter 9204 or modification under Chapter 9204 or 9205),
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide detailed instructions on the manner in which the documents are to be submitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State that Freddie Mac must receive the requested file documentation within 30 calendar
elements.label: text

elements.text: days from the date of the letter requesting the documentation or such other time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac specifies in the request
elements.label: text

elements.text: All documentation sent to Freddie Mac may be used in the File Review, even if the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes documentation that is not required. Freddie Mac may request additional Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: files and/or additional documents during the File Review. The Servicer must submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested documentation in the manner specified by Freddie Mac, or Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: refuse to accept the documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer’s failure to submit requested documentation will be considered a violation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, and Freddie Mac will pursue all rights and remedies available under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents and applicable law. Additionally, failure to submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation is considered a file defect and may be considered in determining a Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall performance in accordance with Section 3501.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Review conclusions and appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon completion of the File Review, Freddie Mac will provide its conclusions, including any
elements.label: text

elements.text: defects, in writing to the Servicer. Servicers may provide Freddie Mac with a response or
elements.label: text

elements.text: appeal to the findings noted in Freddie Mac’s conclusions up to 15 Business Days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of Freddie Mac’s written conclusions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an identified defect was the result of a missing or incorrect document, that document may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be submitted with the Servicer’s appeal to support clearing the defect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer failed to submit any documents in response to Freddie Mac’s initial File
elements.label: text

elements.text: Review request on a Mortgage, and the overall result is “Fail – Missing Docs,” the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not appeal the defect for that Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s decision on the File Review shall be deemed conclusive and may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered as a factor in determining a Servicer’s overall performance in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3501.2. Freddie Mac, in its sole and absolute discretion, may choose to rely on such
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusions or upon any other available information in determining whether to pursue any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other right or remedy available to Freddie Mac under the Purchase Documents or applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: File Reviews are not a substitute for the Servicer’s own internal quality control for
elements.label: text

elements.text: default management activities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3501.2: Servicer performance metrics (11/19/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer performance results
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Servicer Honors and Rewards Program (SHARP)SM
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer Success Scorecard is a confidential monthly performance review for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that measures a Servicer’s performance based on key metrics in certain categories,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as investor reporting and default management.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer’s performance results are published monthly in a Servicer Success Scorecard at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/pdf/step-by-step-guides/serv_success_prog.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer Success Scorecard performance metrics, and changes thereto by Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: are incorporated into and made a part of the Guide and other Purchase Documents by this
elements.label: text

elements.text: reference. Certain Servicers may have individual performance goals based on the metrics in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer Success Scorecard that are incorporated into their Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to amend the performance metrics, modify how the rankings
elements.label: text

elements.text: are determined (as described below) and revise the content of the Servicer Success Scorecard
elements.label: text

elements.text: or SHARP (as described below) at any time. Each Servicer Success Scorecard is considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be “confidential information” for purposes of Section 1201.8.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information on Freddie Mac’s default management or investor reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance categories, see Sections 9201.5 and 8301.1(c), respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer performance results
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will regularly monitor each Servicer’s performance against the metrics in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Success Scorecard. Servicers will be placed into groups based upon similarities in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers’ portfolios (each a “rank group”). The rank groups will be determined based upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: portfolio composition parameters determined by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certain rank groups, the Servicer Success Scorecard will provide a Servicer with overall
elements.label: text

elements.text: rankings, monthly and annual (for that calendar year), as applicable, compared to other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers in such Servicer’s rank group in the default management category.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer will be presumed to have an unacceptable Servicer Success Scorecard result if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s overall monthly or annual ranking in the default management category is in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: bottom 25% of ranked Servicers in the Servicer’s rank group.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac considers the following factors in evaluating the Servicer’s overall performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: when evaluating for potential disqualification or suspension:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Trends in performance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adequacy of staffing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Audit results,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer Success Scorecard results,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer Success File Reviews, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with all requirements of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) SHARP
elements.label: text

elements.text: For details on SHARP, Freddie Mac’s rewards and recognition program based on Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance results, visit the Freddie Mac SHARP webpage at:
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/servicing/products￾programs/sharp/overview.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3601: Remedies (Including Repurchase
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and Termination of Servicing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3601.1: Freddie Mac remedies (11/19/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remedies and related requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occurrence of a Claims Event; resulting liability immediately due and owing to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Remedies and related requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to any other remedies it may have at law or in equity or elsewhere in any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Document, for any Mortgage it purchased, Freddie Mac may require the Seller or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to (and the Seller or Servicer agrees to):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indemnify Freddie Mac and hold it harmless for any loss, damage or expense (including
elements.label: text

elements.text: court costs and reasonable attorney fees) that it may sustain
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage at any time if the Borrower or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other party in the Mortgage transaction has made any misstatement, misrepresentation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: omission in conjunction with such transaction, whether or not the Seller or Servicer was a
elements.label: text

elements.text: party to or had knowledge of such false representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage or comply with the terms of any
elements.label: text
