elements.text: 3602.2: Repurchases requirements and alternatives
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchases required by Freddie Mac due to violations of sale representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchases and repurchase alternatives required by Freddie Mac due to Servicing violations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchases requested by the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Repurchases required by Freddie Mac due to violations of sale representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Joint and several liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac, the Seller, the Seller as Servicer, any prior Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or the current Transferee Servicer of the Mortgages (referred to as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Seller/Servicer” in this section) are jointly and severally liable for all selling and
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination representations, covenants and warranties in the Purchase Documents with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to the Mortgages, unless specifically otherwise provided in the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to any other remedies it may have at law or in equity for any Mortgage it
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased, Freddie Mac may require the Seller/Servicer to repurchase Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in a Mortgage if Freddie Mac has designated any loan-level deficiency that
elements.label: text

elements.text: breaches a term contained in the Purchase Documents in effect at the time of Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase as resulting in a Significant Defect (as such term is defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Repurchase requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will require the Seller/Servicer to repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage if the repurchase is required under the terms of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s decision to require the Seller/Servicer to repurchase a Mortgage shall be
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusive. Failure to comply with Freddie Mac’s repurchase requirement may result in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suspension of selling and/or Servicing privileges or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disqualification as a Freddie Mac Seller and/or Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Suspension or disqualification shall not limit Freddie Mac’s right to take other action to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforce its rights or protect its interests.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Time frame for repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in the identified Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 60 days of the date of Freddie Mac’s request or within such other time frame as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any appeal of Freddie Mac’s Repurchase Demand must be made in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 3602.4(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Repurchase alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, at its sole discretion, provide the Seller/Servicer with a Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative (as such term is defined in Section 3401.1(a)(i)) of the identified Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 3401.1(a)(vi). In each such case, Freddie Mac will notify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer of the type and terms of the Repurchase Alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this Section 3602.2(a), Mortgages that are subject to repurchase include
elements.label: text

elements.text: active Mortgages, inactive Mortgages and REO and all other post-foreclosure situations
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the provisions of Section 3602.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Repurchases and repurchase alternatives required by Freddie Mac due to Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: violations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Process for remediating Servicing Violations and related Servicing Defects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The steps below describe the process that Freddie Mac will follow to categorize
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Defects, allow Servicing Corrections to Servicing Defects through a notice of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Defect and remedy Servicing Defects through a Servicing Remedy letter (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Demand or Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Determination of whether to send a notice of Servicing Defect or a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac identifies a Servicing Violation, Freddie Mac may issue either a notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicing Defect or a Servicing Remedy letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will issue a notice of Servicing Defect or a Servicing Remedy letter for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: title-related defect only after liquidation of the Mortgage (i.e., after foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: following completion of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure) unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A prior foreclosure has extinguished Freddie Mac’s Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has determined that the title-related defect prevents foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other enforcement of Freddie Mac’s rights under the Note or Mortgage (e.g., the
elements.label: text

elements.text: title-related defect prevents completion of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Notice of Servicing Defect
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines that the Servicing Defect resulting from a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Violation can be reasonably corrected by the Servicer, Freddie Mac will issue a
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice of Servicing Defect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Servicing Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac deems the Servicing Defect resulting from a Servicing Violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be uncorrectable by the Servicer, Freddie Mac will issue a Servicing Remedy
elements.label: text

elements.text: letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicing Remedy letter may also be issued if a Servicing Defect (i) is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: corrected by the Servicer during the Servicing Correction period, if applicable or
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) caused or will cause Freddie Mac losses, expenses or damages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: notwithstanding any Servicing Correction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer fails to comply with any Servicing Remedy letter, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue other available rights and remedies under the Guide and the other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, including repurchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are limited instances in which a Servicing Defect resulting from a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Violation may be deemed to be uncorrectable. Therefore, a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedy letter may be issued in lieu of a notice of Servicing Defect when a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Defect:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Extinguishes the lien, security interest or other property interest, or the lien,
elements.label: text

elements.text: security interest or other property interest becomes inadequate for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: realization of the benefits of the security against the related Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Causes irreparable damage to the physical improvements to the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises or REO property or renders the Mortgaged Premises or REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: property uninhabitable;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is a result of a foreclosure sale to a third-party purchaser, completed short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: or completed deed-in-lieu of foreclosure that was not compliant with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and the other Purchase Documents;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Extinguishes Freddie Mac’s ability to either file an insurance claim or seek
elements.label: text

elements.text: full recovery of an insurance claim amount, for any insurance or guarantee
elements.label: text

elements.text: type; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Results in a property that was not preserved and maintained in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Guide and the other Purchase Documents, and, following acquisition
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the property, Freddie Mac needs to make any repairs to the REO property
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a result
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter will be issued for Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defects not constituting Servicing Repurchase Defects, including, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: after a Servicing Correction period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will issue any such Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 60 days after the expiration of the Servicing Correction period, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: any extensions and resolution of any appeals (see Section 3602.4(c)) unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac provides notice to the Servicer that it is unable to provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter within such time frame. Any
elements.label: text

elements.text: such notice will describe the anticipated time frame for issuing the related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Repurchase Demand
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Repurchase Demand will be issued for any Servicing Repurchase Defect. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of this Section 3602.2(b), Repurchase Demands include active
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, inactive Mortgages and REO and all other post-foreclosure situations
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the provisions of Section 3602.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are multiple Servicing Defects caused by a Servicing Violation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Violations, Freddie Mac may either issue a:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchase Demand if any Servicing Violation constitutes a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Defect, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter for all Servicing Defects, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: each case based on each specific Servicing Violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Contents of notices of Servicing Defect and Servicing Remedy letters
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notice of Servicing Defect or Servicing Remedy letter will include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The specific Servicing Violation(s) and/or related Servicing Defect(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a notice of Servicing Defect, the Servicing Correction period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter, the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative amount, including calculation of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative amount. (For a repurchase request, refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3602.3(a) for details regarding the calculation of the repurchase price.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Servicing Remedy letter: The time frame for completing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase or payment of the Servicing Repurchase Alternative and
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding the Servicer’s right to appeal and time frame for appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 3602.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Servicing Corrections
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the Servicing Correction period identified in a notice of Servicing Defect, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must correct the related Servicing Defect(s) in the specified time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in the notice of Servicing Defect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Following the Servicing Correction period, Freddie Mac will assess any Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correction made by the Servicer to determine whether a Servicing Remedy letter will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be issued. If the Servicer provides an acceptable Servicing Correction, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will not pursue a repurchase; however, Freddie Mac may still issue a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative demand letter for any damages, expenses or losses suffered as
elements.label: text

elements.text: a result of the Servicing Violation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines it will not issue a Servicing Remedy letter, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will notify the Servicer that the related notice of Servicing Defect has been closed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may attempt to correct the Servicing Defect identified in a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedy letter following the expiration of the Servicing Correction period and
elements.label: text

elements.text: issuance of the Servicing Remedy letter; however, once the Appeal Process has
elements.label: text

elements.text: concluded or, if applicable, the impasse period has expired, as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3602.4(c), the Servicer can no longer submit a Servicing Correction for consideration
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Appeals of Servicing Remedy letters
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Servicing Remedy letters issued to a Servicer are subject to the Appeal Process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which may be initiated by the Servicer in accordance with Section 3602.4(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Other representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to any other remedies it may have at law or in equity for any Mortgage it
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased, Freddie Mac may require the Servicer to repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Mortgage if, in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, Freddie Mac determines that the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: has failed to comply with any contractual representation and warranty, as prescribed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide and the other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contractual representations and warranties include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with Chapter 3201 regarding fraud,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with the Freddie Mac Charter Act,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Warranties and obligations of the Servicer regarding the Freddie Mac Exclusionary
elements.label: text

elements.text: List or FHFA‘s Suspended Counterparty Program, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with applicable law, all as related to the Servicing of a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Repurchases requested by the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under exceptional circumstances, the Seller/Servicer may be allowed to repurchase Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s interest in a Mortgage. Each repurchase must have Freddie Mac’s prior written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval and be documented in the applicable Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To request a repurchase, the Seller/Servicer must complete Form 105, Multipurpose Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Transmittal, and submit it to Freddie Mac via e-mail at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase@FreddieMac.com. (See Directory 1 for additional submission options.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Generally, only a Mortgage that is 90 or more days delinquent or being foreclosed upon may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be repurchased under this section. Without Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may not voluntarily repurchase a Price-Adjusted Loan (PAL) (as such term is
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 3401.1(a)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3602.3: Repurchase pricing and documentation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchase pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting and reporting repurchases
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Repurchase pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The repurchase price is determined based on the Mortgage status as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Active Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: An active Mortgage is a current or delinquent Mortgage for which the Servicer reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Freddie Mac drafts payments and payoffs. The repurchase price is calculated as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UPB of the Mortgage, including any Negative Amortization on an ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued interest at the applicable net yield rate from the first day of the month of
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase through the day before repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Inactive Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: An active Mortgage becomes an inactive Mortgage in the Accounting Cycle it becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 120 days delinquent (four payments past due). The repurchase price is calculated as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UPB of the Mortgage, including any Negative Amortization on an ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued interest at the applicable net yield rate from the DDLPI through the day
elements.label: text

elements.text: before repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any expenses reimbursed by Freddie Mac to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other costs incurred by Freddie Mac and the Servicer in connection with
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or the liquidation of the Mortgage in default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any mortgage insurance proceeds, sale proceeds, other proceeds, rental income or
elements.label: text

elements.text: refunds remitted to Freddie Mac by or on behalf of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)REO and all other post-foreclosure situations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The repurchase price is calculated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UPB of the Mortgage as of the acquisition date or the date of acceptance of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, including any Negative Amortization on an ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued interest at the:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Accounting Net Yield (ANY) from the DDLPI through the earlier of the day
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the repurchase due date or the 150th day from the DDLPI
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Average of the prime rate for the month preceding the Repurchase Demand date
elements.label: text

elements.text: minus one half of 1% from the 151st day from the DDLPI through the earlier of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the day before the repurchase due date or 60 days after the settlement date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale of the REO (if applicable)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any expenses reimbursed by Freddie Mac to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All costs associated with the acquisition, maintenance, rehabilitation and disposition
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the property acquired, including any incentive or below market financing provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac and the Servicer of the Mortgage in connection with the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An allocation for general and administrative expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other cost incurred by Freddie Mac and the Servicer in connection with the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, foreclosure or the liquidation of Mortgages in default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The cost of advancing payment for expenses net of any proceeds received at the:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  ANY from the DDLPI through the earlier of the day before the repurchase due
elements.label: text

elements.text: date on the 150th day from the DDLPI
elements.label: text

elements.text:  The average of the prime rate for the month preceding the Repurchase Demand
elements.label: text

elements.text: date minus one half of 1% from the 151st day from the DDLPI through the earlier
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the day before the repurchase due date or 60 days after the settlement date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale of the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minus
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any mortgage insurance proceeds, sale proceeds, other proceeds, rental income or
elements.label: text

elements.text: refunds remitted to Freddie Mac by or on behalf of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees that, in the event of repurchase, it will pay all documentary stamp
elements.label: text

elements.text: taxes, recording fees, transfer taxes and all other expenses payable in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At Freddie Mac’s discretion, the repurchase price may also include any premium paid for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages Freddie Mac purchased or, with respect to Mortgage delivery through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guarantor program, the premium (expressed as a percentage) that was or would have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable to the Pool comprising the particular Mortgages, based on market conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing on the Settlement Date as determined by Freddie Mac, multiplied by the outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB of the particular Mortgage on the repurchase date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the repurchase price will include an amount equal to any buyup proceeds paid by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to the Seller in connection with the sale of the Mortgage to Freddie Mac and an
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount equal to any loss, damage or expense, including court costs and reasonable attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees, as incurred by Freddie Mac in connection with its purchase, ownership and resale to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must remit the repurchase funds or comply with the repurchase appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 3602.4(b) or 3602.4(c), as applicable, within 60 days of the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s repurchase letter. If the Seller/Servicer does not submit a written appeal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac within 60 days or within such other time frame as specified by Freddie Mac, it
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be assumed that the Seller/Servicer does not contest the repurchase and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase funds are due to Freddie Mac. Thereupon the Appeal Process will no longer be
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to the Seller/Servicer for that particular Repurchase Demand.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer fails to report an involuntary repurchase in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.3(f) and the late reported payoff-repurchase results in a delayed Payoff Date or if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer fails to have sufficient funds available for Freddie Mac to draft on the Payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: Draft Date, then the Seller/Servicer may be charged a draft delay fee each month on the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount owed. The rate of interest on the late remittance amount will equal the highest quoted
elements.label: text

elements.text: prime rate printed on the last Business Day following the month in which the repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds are due plus 3% (300 basis points).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The prime rate is published in The Wall Street Journal’s “Money Rates” column or
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable section. If the prime rate is not published, Freddie Mac will determine a
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable rate. The amount of any repurchase late fee will appear on the Seller account
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity statement described in Section 6303.1(a) and will be drafted from the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Clearing House (ACH) account in accordance with Section 6303.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 8303.5(h) for details on how Freddie Mac calculates the draft delay fee and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee structure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documenting and reporting repurchases
elements.label: text

elements.text: All repurchases must be documented in the applicable Mortgage files and are subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac review. In addition, for any repurchase made to allow a Transfer of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Section 3602.2(b), the Seller/Servicer must also retain the documentation required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8406.3(a) after the transfer has occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchases, other than those for which the Seller/Servicer has received written demand by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, must have Freddie Mac’s prior written approval. (See Section 3602.2(c) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on submitting a request to repurchase a Mortgage.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A repurchase is not completed until the reporting and remitting requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.3(f) have been satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3602.4: Appeal and dispute resolution processes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Survival of Freddie Mac’s remedies; misrepresentations by the Seller or Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appealing a Repurchase Demand due to violations of selling and origination representations
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appealing a Repurchase Demand or repurchase alternative issued due to Servicing violations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Survival of Freddie Mac’s remedies; misrepresentations by the Seller or Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s decision to require or allow a repurchase in no way diminishes its right to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursue further action such as disqualification, suspension or termination, in whole or in part,
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights under the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2301 and/or 3603. Freddie Mac may also exercise these remedies when its
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection of the documentation of a repurchased Mortgage reveals facts materially different
elements.label: text

elements.text: from those for which Freddie Mac originally approved this repurchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appealing a Repurchase Demand due to violations of selling and origination
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of a Repurchase Demand for a violation, if the Seller/Servicer has additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting information and/or documentation that may affect Freddie Mac’s decision, they
elements.label: text

elements.text: may file an appeal. See Section 3401.1(a) for information regarding Freddie Mac’s review of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections submitted by the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) First appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 60 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s Repurchase Demand or within such other
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frame as may be specified by Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer may submit the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: appeal. The first Appeal Process is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The appeal must be sent in writing to the Freddie Mac office requesting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Seller/Servicer’s appeal package must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement of all relevant facts concerning the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An explanation of why these facts were not disclosed in the file during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement of why Freddie Mac’s decision should be reversed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All available documentation supporting the basis for the first appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the Seller/Servicer submits a timely appeal, Freddie Mac will review the appeal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: advise the Seller/Servicer in writing of the appeal decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. At any time during the first Appeal Process, the Seller/Servicer has the right to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide a Correction of an alleged Significant Defect (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the first appeal is denied, the Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with the terms of the Repurchase Demand within 15 days from the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac’s denial letter or within such other time frame as specified by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a second appeal as set forth in Section 3602.4(b)(ii) below
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. In order for Freddie Mac to have the right to seek a remedy based on the breach(es)
elements.label: text

elements.text: alleged in the Repurchase Demand, Freddie Mac must respond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s first written appeal within 60 days of receipt of the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: written appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a first appeal is not received within the 60-day period or within such other time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: as may be specified by Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer will have no further right to
elements.label: text

elements.text: challenge such Repurchase Demand and will not be able to avail itself of the Impasse
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process, the Management Escalation Process or the IDR Process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Second appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: If information that was not available at the time of the first appeal (and was therefore not
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted to Freddie Mac for consideration) becomes available after submission of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first appeal, the Seller/Servicer may submit an additional appeal (“second appeal”) of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Demand in writing within 15 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s denial
elements.label: text

elements.text: letter of the first appeal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The second appeal must provide new material documentation or information that was not
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously available and must contain the items described in Section 3602.4(b)(i)(2). The
elements.label: text

elements.text: second Appeal Process is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Freddie Mac will review the second appeal and attempt to resolve the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. At any time during the second Appeal Process, the Seller/Servicer may provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Correction of an alleged Significant Defect as set forth in the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the second appeal is denied, the Seller/Servicer must, within 15 days from the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac’s denial letter, initiate the Impasse Process pursuant to the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3602.4(b)(iii) or implement the remedy requested by the Repurchase Demand
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the Impasse Process is not initiated within the 15-day period, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have no further right to challenge the Repurchase Demand,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be able to avail itself of the Impasse Process, the Management Escalation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process or the IDR Process in connection with the Mortgage in question, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be obligated to timely comply with the terms of the Repurchase Demand
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. In order for Freddie Mac to have the right to seek a remedy based on the breach(es)
elements.label: text

elements.text: alleged in the Repurchase Demand, Freddie Mac must respond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s first written appeal within 60 days of receipt of the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: written appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: If new material information is not available after submission of the first appeal, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may initiate the Impasse Process (described in Section 3602.4(b)(iii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: below) within 15 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s denial letter of the first appeal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Impasse Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer wishes to challenge the Repurchase Demand after conclusion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appeal Process, the Seller/Servicer must initiate the Impasse Process through a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: request to Freddie Mac requesting such escalation within 15 days of the date of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s denial of the first or second appeal, as applicable. The requirements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Impasse Process are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The request for such escalation must be sent in writing to the Freddie Mac office that
elements.label: text

elements.text: denied the prior appeal. If any new information is available, the request must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: the items described in Section 3602.4(b)(i)(2) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Within 30 days (or such longer period of time to which both parties agree) from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date the Impasse Process is initiated (the “impasse period”), a representative from
elements.label: text

elements.text: each of the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac will attempt to resolve the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller/Servicer may provide a Correction of an alleged Significant Defect at any
elements.label: text

elements.text: time during the impasse period
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. All information, facts and documents that the Seller/Servicer or Freddie Mac wishes
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be considered during the Impasse Process must be submitted no later than the end
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the impasse period, or such information will not be considered in the Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escalation Process, any subsequent IDR Process or any other attempts to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the parties agree during the impasse period that no breach occurred, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will withdraw the Repurchase Demand. If the parties agree during the impasse period
elements.label: text

elements.text: that a breach did occur, the Seller/Servicer will implement the remedy specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Demand (or such other remedy as agreed to by the parties).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. If, after the impasse period, Freddie Mac reaffirms its Repurchase Demand, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may continue to dispute the Repurchase Demand by initiating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management Escalation Process within 15 days of receiving Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: reaffirmation of its decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If the Management Escalation Process is not initiated within this 15-day period, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have no further right to challenge the Repurchase Demand,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be able to avail itself of the Management Escalation Process or the IDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be obligated to comply with the terms of the Repurchase Demand within 15 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the end of the impasse period
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. The Impasse Process is available only for Repurchase Demands concerning
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with Settlement Dates on and after January 1, 2016
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Management Escalation Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the dispute is not resolved by the Appeal Process or the Impasse Process, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may initiate the Management Escalation Process by submitting a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: request to Freddie Mac no later than 15 days from the end of the impasse period. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for the Management Escalation Process are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Seller/Servicer’s request shall identify its officer contact for the Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escalation Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. No new information, facts or documents or Corrections may be submitted by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, and no new information, facts or documents may be submitted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as part of the Management Escalation Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Within 30 days (or such longer time period to which both parties may agree) after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer initiates the Management Escalation Process (the “management
elements.label: text

elements.text: escalation period”), an officer outside of Freddie Mac’s Quality Control group and an
elements.label: text

elements.text: officer of the Seller/Servicer will attempt to resolve the dispute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer reach agreement regarding the Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Demand in question during the management escalation period, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdraw the Repurchase Demand, or the Seller/Servicer will implement the remedy
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in the Repurchase Demand (or such other remedy as agreed to by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac and the Seller/Servicer) no later than 30 days after an agreement is reached.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the dispute is not resolved by the end of the management escalation period, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must initiate the IDR Process within 15 days of the expiration of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: management escalation period to continue to attempt to resolve the dispute. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer does not initiate the IDR Process within this 15-day period, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have no further right to challenge the Repurchase Demand,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be able to avail itself of the IDR Process in connection with the Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: question, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be obligated to comply with the terms of the Repurchase Demand within 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the end of the management escalation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Management Escalation Process is available only for Repurchase Demands
elements.label: text

elements.text: concerning Mortgages with Settlement Dates on and after January 1, 2016
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) IDR Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: The IDR Process is available to resolve factual disputes regarding Repurchase Demands
elements.label: text

elements.text: that involve an alleged breach of a selling and/or origination representation and warranty.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements for the IDR Process are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. If the dispute is not resolved by the Management Escalation Process, an IDR Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may initiate the IDR Process by following the steps set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3.2 of the IDR Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. As a prerequisite to using the IDR Process, the Seller/Servicer must have timely
elements.label: text

elements.text: challenged the Repurchase Demand through the Appeal Process, the Impasse Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Management Escalation Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the Seller/Servicer has not initiated the IDR Process within 15 days of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of the management escalation period, Freddie Mac may initiate the IDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process within six months of the expiration of the management escalation period by
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the steps set forth in Section 3.2 of the IDR Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the IDR Process is selected by the Seller/Servicer or Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac must both execute the tolling agreement tolling any
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable statute of limitations and statute of repose. The tolling agreement shall be
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed by the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac within seven days of initiation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IDR Process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Once either party initiates the IDR Process, both the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall follow the process set forth in the IDR Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. No new information, facts or documents or Corrections may be submitted by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, and no new information, facts or documents may be submitted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as part of the IDR Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Appealing a Repurchase Demand or repurchase alternative issued due to Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: violations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of a Repurchase Demand or a Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: for any Servicing Violation or other violation described in Section 3602.2(b), the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may initiate the processes set forth herein if the Servicer has additional supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and/or documentation that may affect:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s decision to exercise a Servicing Remedy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s determination that a Servicing Defect is uncorrectable or is a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Defect pursuant to Section 3602.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The processes to resolve a dispute include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Appeal Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Impasse Process and Management Escalation Process (the “escalation processes”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The IDR Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the processes set forth herein to resolve a dispute are not initiated timely, the procedures in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this section will be unavailable to the Servicer, and the Servicer will be obliged to remit
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment for the repurchase or Servicing Repurchase Alternative. Additionally, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s decision in the processes above will be conclusive unless the IDR Process (described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 3602.4(c)(iii) below) is initiated, in which case the decision of the Neutral (as such
elements.label: text

elements.text: term is defined in the IDR Document) shall be binding.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) The Appeal Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 60 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s letter requiring repurchase or providing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Repurchase Alternative, or within such other time frame as may be specified by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, the Servicer may submit the first appeal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If no written appeal is received within the 60-day period or within such other time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: as may be specified by Freddie Mac, the Servicer will have no further right to challenge
elements.label: text

elements.text: such repurchase or Servicing Repurchase Alternative and will not be able to avail itself of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Impasse Process, the Management Escalation Process or the IDR Process. The Appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The first appeal must be sent in writing to the Freddie Mac office referenced in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The appeal package must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement of all relevant facts concerning the Servicing of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement of why Freddie Mac’s decision should be reversed, in whole or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: part
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All available documentation supporting the basis for the appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the Servicer submits a timely appeal, Freddie Mac will review the first appeal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: advise the Servicer in writing of the appeal decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the first appeal is denied, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must remit payment for the repurchase or Servicing Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative within 15 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s denial letter or within
elements.label: text

elements.text: such other time frame as may be specified by Freddie Mac, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If new material information that was not available at the time of the first appeal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and was therefore not submitted to Freddie Mac for consideration, becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: available after submission of the first appeal, the Servicer may submit an
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional appeal (“second appeal”) of the request within 15 days from the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s denial letter. The second appeal must be sent in writing to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac office that denied the prior appeal and must contain the items
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 3602.4(c)(i)(2) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If new material information is not available, the Servicer may initiate the Impasse
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process (described in Section 3602.4(c)(ii) below) within 15 days from the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s denial letter of the first appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. At any time during the Appeal Process, the Servicer has the right to provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Correction pursuant to Section 3602.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. In order for Freddie Mac to have the right to seek a Servicing Remedy based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: breach(es) alleged in the Repurchase Demand or Servicing Repurchase Alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: demand letter, Freddie Mac must respond to the Servicer’s first written appeal (and
elements.label: text

elements.text: second written appeal, as applicable) within 60 days of receipt of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: written appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If there is a second appeal and the second appeal is denied, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must remit payment for the repurchase or Servicing Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative within 15 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s denial letter or within
elements.label: text

elements.text: such other time frame as may be specified by Freddie Mac, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer may initiate the Impasse Process within 15 days from the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s denial letter of the second appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. If the Impasse Process is not initiated timely following denial of the first or second
elements.label: text

elements.text: appeal, the Servicer will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have no further right to challenge the Repurchase Demand or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative demand letter,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be able to avail itself of the Impasse Process, the Management Escalation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process or the IDR Process in connection with the Mortgage in question, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be obligated to timely comply with the terms of the Servicing Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. In the event the first or second appeal results in the determination that a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defect can be corrected, or the Servicer provided a Servicing Correction during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appeal Process, the Servicing Remedy letter will be considered withdrawn, and, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, Freddie Mac will issue a notice of Servicing Defect as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3602.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. If the first or second appeal and denial of such appeal was in response to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s notification to the Servicer regarding a Servicing Repurchase Alternative,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not consider any further appeal if Freddie Mac subsequently
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests repurchase and the Repurchase Demand is due to the same violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified in the Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) The escalation processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)The Impasse Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer wishes to challenge the Servicing Remedy letter after conclusion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appeal Process, the Servicer must initiate the Impasse Process though a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: request to Freddie Mac requesting such escalation within 15 days from the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s denial letter for the first or second appeal, as applicable. The Impasse
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The request for such escalation must be sent in writing to the Freddie Mac office
elements.label: text

elements.text: that denied the prior appeal. If any new information is available, the request must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the items described in Section 3602.4(c)(i)(2) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Within 30 days (or such longer period of time to which both parties agree) from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the date the Impasse Process is initiated (the “impasse period”), a representative
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Servicer and a representative from Freddie Mac will attempt to resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Servicer may provide a Servicing Correction pursuant to Section 3602.2(b) at
elements.label: text

elements.text: any time during the impasse period
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. All information, facts and documents that the Servicer or Freddie Mac wishes to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be considered during the Impasse Process must be submitted no later than the end
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the impasse period, or such information will not be considered in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management Escalation Process, any subsequent IDR Process or any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: attempts to resolve the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the parties agree during the impasse period that no Servicing Violation occurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: or that no Servicing Defect exists, the Servicing Remedy letter will be considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdrawn. If the parties agree that a Servicing Defect can be corrected or that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer provided a Servicing Correction during the impasse period, the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remedy letter will be considered withdrawn, and, if applicable, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: issue a notice of Servicing Defect described in Section 3602.2(b). If the parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: agree during the impasse period that a Servicing Violation occurred or that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Defect exists, the Servicer will implement the remedy specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Demand or Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. If, after the impasse period, Freddie Mac reaffirms its Servicing Remedy letter,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer may continue to dispute the Servicing Remedy letter by initiating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management Escalation Process within 15 days of receiving Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: reaffirmation of its decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If the Management Escalation Process is not initiated within this 15-day period,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have no further right to challenge the Repurchase Demand or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative demand letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be able to avail itself of the Management Escalation Process or the IDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process in connection with the Mortgage in question, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be obligated to timely comply with the terms of the Servicing Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) The Management Escalation Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the dispute is not resolved by the Appeal Process or the Impasse Process, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may initiate the Management Escalation Process by submitting a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: request to Freddie Mac no later than 15 days of receiving Freddie Mac’s reaffirmation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of its decision. The Management Escalation Process is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The request for such escalation must be sent in writing to the Freddie Mac office
elements.label: text

elements.text: referenced in the letter reaffirming the Servicing Remedy, and the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify its officer contact for the Management Escalation Process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. No new information, facts or documents, or Servicing Corrections pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3602.2(b), may be submitted by the Servicer, and no new information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: facts or documents may be submitted by Freddie Mac as part of the Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escalation Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Within 30 days (or such longer period of time to which both parties may agree)
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Servicer initiates the Management Escalation Process (“management
elements.label: text

elements.text: escalation period”), an officer from the Servicer and Freddie Mac will attempt to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolve the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the parties agree during the management escalation period that no Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Violation occurred or that no Servicing Defect exists, the Servicing Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be considered withdrawn. If the parties agree during the management
elements.label: text

elements.text: escalation period that a Servicing Violation occurred or that a Servicing Defect
elements.label: text

elements.text: exists, the Servicer will implement the remedy specified in the Servicing Remedy
elements.label: text

elements.text: letter no later than 30 days after an agreement is reached.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the dispute is not resolved by the end of the management escalation period and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the dispute is related to Freddie Mac’s determination that a Servicing Defect
elements.label: text

elements.text: exists, the Servicer must initiate the IDR Process within 15 days of the expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the management escalation period to continue to attempt to resolve the dispute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer does not initiate the IDR process within this 15-day period, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have no further right to challenge the Repurchase Demand or Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repurchase Alternative demand letter,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be able to avail itself of the IDR Process in connection with the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: in question, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be obligated to timely comply with the terms of the Servicing Remedy letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)The IDR Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: The IDR Process is available to resolve factual disputes regarding Servicing Remedy
elements.label: text

elements.text: letters that involve an alleged Servicing Defect. The IDR Process will only address loan￾level Servicing Remedy letters and whether the Servicing Defect exists at the time the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IDR Process commences. The IDR Process will not address Servicing Remedies or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other issue or dispute. The IDR Process is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. If the Servicer disputes Freddie Mac’s determination that a Servicing Defect exists,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the dispute is not resolved by the Management Escalation Process, an IDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Seller/Servicer may initiate the IDR Process within 15 days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of the management escalation period. The IDR Process and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: are set forth in the IDR Document.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. As a prerequisite to using the IDR Process, the Servicer must have timely challenged
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Repurchase Demand or the Servicing Repurchase Alternative demand letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the Appeal Process, the Impasse Process and the Management Escalation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the Servicer has not initiated the IDR Process within 15 days of the expiration of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the management escalation period, Freddie Mac may initiate the IDR Process within
elements.label: text

elements.text: six months of the expiration of the management escalation period by following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: steps in Section 3.2 of the IDR Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the IDR Process is elected by the Servicer or Freddie Mac, the Servicer and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac must both execute the tolling agreement tolling any applicable statute of
elements.label: text
